rulings.text: Marbury v. Madison, 5 U.S. (1 Cranch) 137 (1803): the judicial power includes the authority to declare acts of Congress repugnant to the Constitution void.
Gideon v. Wainwright, 372 U.S. 335 (1963) held that indigent defendants are entitled to appointed counsel in all criminal prosecutions, including petty offenses.
Thompson v. Meridian Data Sys., 847 F.4th 1122 (9th Cir. 2019)
